free_text: Excitation functions and isomeric cross-section
ratios of (d,xn) reactions on 86Sr
88 Inch Cyclotron
The enriched 86Sr material was provided as 86SrCO3
powder. Thin strontium carbonate samples were
prepared by the sedimentation technique. A 50 mu-m
thick Al foil (chemical purity 99.0%) was used as the
backing for sedimentation. The net weight of the
sediments lay in the range of 5 to 7 mg. Each
sedimented sample was then covered by a 10 mu-m thick
Al foil of 16 mm diameter, welted around the backing
foil.
The energy resolution (FWHM) at 1332.5 keV of
60Co of the HPGe detectors used was 2.5 keV. For
efficiency calibration of the detector, standard point
sources were used 54Mn, 133Ba, 137Cs and 152Eu.
Thin foils of Al, Ti, Fe and Cu of natural isotopic
composition (purity: Al (99.0%); Ti (>99.6%);
Fe (99.6%); Cu (99.9%), thickness of both Fe and Ti
foils - 25 mu-m, Al foil - 50 and 250 mu-m, Cu foil -
50 mu-m) were used as beam monitors and inserted into
the stack. Each foil was cut in circular disc with a
diameter of 13 mm.
Statistical uncertainty
Uncertainty in detector efficiency
Uncertainty in true coincidence correction
Uncertainty in gamma-ray intensities
Uncertainty in half-life
Uncertainty in deuteron flux
Uncertainty in sample homogeneity
Total uncertainty

Contributions from isotopes 87Sr, 88Sr were calculated
by TALYS code and calculated contribution was
subtracted from the measured cross sections.
Correction on contribution to 87Y-M through the small
amount of 87Sr and 88Sr in the sample was done.
Correction factor contains the ratio of the
corrected and uncorrected cross sections.
Tab. 2

Correction on contribution through the small amount of
87Sr and 88Sr in the sample was not done.
The formation from low-abundant 87Sr and 88Sr in the
target was estimated through model calculation as <5%
and <1%, respectively.
